_comment: Worked example — Notification Preferences. Use this as the canonical shape reference when generating backlog JSON for Apex. featureFlag is present here because this feature uses controlled rollout; omit featureFlag entirely on Features when the PRD ## Feature Flag section has Flag required: No.

personas.name: Authenticated User
personas.description: Any logged-in Apex user who receives notifications and wants to control their preferences

personas.name: Platform Admin
personas.description: Administrator who manages platform-wide notification settings and defaults

businessRules.rule: A user may only modify their own notification preferences
Platform-wide notification defaults are set by Platform Admin and apply to all new users

epics.title: Notification Preferences
epics.description: Enable users to control which notifications they receive and how they are delivered. Currently all notifications are sent to all users with no opt-out mechanism, leading to notification fatigue and reduced engagement with important alerts.
successMetrics: 80% of active users configure at least one preference within 30 days of release
assumptions: Notification types are stable and do not change frequently

features.title: Configure Personal Notification Preferences
features.description: Authenticated Users will be able to toggle notifications on/off per notification type and choose whether toast alerts appear for each type.
dependencies: Notification type registry must be queryable from the server

userStory.iWant: toggle individual notification types on or off
userStory.soThat: I only receive notifications relevant to my work

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Preference save must complete within 1 second
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Toggle controls must be keyboard-navigable and screen-reader labeled
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: Endpoint must validate user identity before saving preferences

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am on the Notification Preferences page
acceptanceCriteria.when: I toggle a notification type off
acceptanceCriteria.then: The preference is saved and I no longer receive notifications of that type

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am on the Notification Preferences page and the save fails
acceptanceCriteria.then: An error toast is shown and the toggle reverts to its previous state

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am a new user with no saved preferences
acceptanceCriteria.when: I visit the Notification Preferences page
acceptanceCriteria.then: All toggles reflect the platform defaults set by the admin

acceptanceCriteria.given: I am on the Notification Preferences page
acceptanceCriteria.when: I attempt to access another user's preferences via API
acceptanceCriteria.then: I receive a 403 error and no changes are made

items.title: Create notification_preferences table and API endpoints
items.description: Create a PostgreSQL migration for the notification_preferences table with user_id, notification_type, enabled, and toast_enabled columns. Add Express API endpoints for GET and PUT operations with user-scoped access control.
technicalDependencies: Drizzle ORM schema update required after migration
nonFunctionalRequirements: Must validate user identity — return 403 on cross-user access
definitionOfDone: Migration creates notification_preferences table with proper indexes
Unit tests cover: success, unauthorized, and not-found scenarios

assumptionsMade: Notification types are a stable enum that does not change per deployment
WCAG 2.1 AA is the agreed accessibility standard for this project
